# PRD: Archetype Skills Implementation

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Status:** Draft
**Requested by:** Z (via Citlali)

---

## 1. Goal

Upgrade the 6 archetype system prompts from generic role descriptions to skill-rich specialists that produce real deliverables, making each director feel like hiring a domain expert rather than chatting with a renamed chatbot.

## 2. Why

The current prompts define personality and expertise but don't guide Claude to produce specific artifacts. A nonprofit ED asking the Development Director to "help with grants" gets a general conversation instead of a ranked opportunity list or a draft LOI. The value proposition of Edify ("six directors, every operational role covered") depends on each director being able to DO things, not just TALK about things.

Z approved this direction on 2026-04-13: "I trust yall. Can yall do implementation?"

## 3. Success criteria

- [ ] Each of the 6 archetype prompts includes personality, signature phrases, and tone (from ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md)
- [ ] Each archetype has 2-3 core skills defined as structured prompt workflows
- [ ] Suggested prompts in team chat UI map to skills (not generic questions)
- [ ] Each skill produces a specific, usable deliverable (not just advice)
- [ ] Finance Director fully removed from all remaining code
- [ ] Build passes clean, Vercel deploys successfully

## 4. Tech stack

- **Framework:** Next.js 14 (static export, client-side BYOK)
- **Files to modify:**
  - `apps/web/src/lib/archetype-prompts.ts` — enhanced system prompts
  - `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/team/[slug]/TeamChatClient.tsx` — updated suggested prompts
  - `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/decision-lab/api.ts` — update to 6 archetypes
- **Architecture note:** All skills run as prompt-engineered workflows via direct Claude API calls from the browser. No backend needed. The "tools" from ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md become structured instructions in the system prompt.

## 5. Scope -- what's IN

### A. Enhanced System Prompts (archetype-prompts.ts)

Rewrite all 6 prompts to include personality profiles, signature phrases, and skill instructions from ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md. Each prompt should be structured as:

1. **Personality** — communication style, tone, decision-making approach, signature phrases
2. **Expertise** — domain knowledge areas
3. **Core Skills** — 2-3 structured workflows with clear artifact outputs
4. **Communication Rules** — anti-sycophancy rules (already present, keep them)
5. **Instructions** — how to handle requests, when to defer to other directors

### B. Core Skills Per Archetype

**1. Development Director**
- **Grant Finder** — When asked about grants, produce a ranked opportunity list with funder name, typical award amount, deadline, mission fit score (1-10), and eligibility notes. Always recommend top 3.
- **Proposal Drafter** — When asked to write a grant section, produce a polished draft (LOI, narrative, budget justification, or evaluation plan) using org context. Include word count and funder-specific formatting.
- **Donor Outreach Writer** — When asked about donor communications, produce ready-to-send emails or letters with personalization based on giving history and engagement level.

**2. Marketing Director**
- **Content Creator** — When asked for content, produce platform-specific ready-to-post copy (LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, email) with correct character limits, hashtags, and CTAs. Always offer 2-3 creative angles.
- **Campaign Planner** — When asked to plan a campaign, produce a content calendar with post topics, formats, channels, and scheduling across a specified time period.
- **Brand Voice Editor** — When given draft text, evaluate against org brand voice and rewrite for consistency. Explain what changed and why.

**3. Executive Assistant**
- **Meeting Prep** — When told about an upcoming meeting, produce a complete meeting packet: agenda with time allocations, attendee brief, key discussion points, and action items from the last meeting.
- **Email Drafter** — When given a communication need, produce a ready-to-send email in the appropriate tone (formal/friendly/brief) with clear action items and next steps.
- **Action Item Tracker** — When given meeting notes or a conversation, extract all action items with owners, deadlines, and priority levels in a structured table.

**4. Programs Director**
- **Logic Model Builder** — When asked to design or evaluate a program, produce a structured logic model: inputs, activities, outputs, short-term outcomes, long-term outcomes. Include target population and measurement indicators.
- **Outcome Reporter** — When asked for a report, produce a funder-ready program narrative with outcome data, participant stories, progress toward targets, and lessons learned.
- **Survey Designer** — When asked for evaluation tools, produce a complete survey instrument (intake, satisfaction, outcome, or exit) with 10-15 questions appropriate to the program and measurement need.

**5. HR & Volunteer Coordinator**
- **Policy Writer** — When asked for HR policies, produce a plain-language policy document ready for review. Include purpose, scope, procedure, and a note flagging anything that warrants legal review.
- **Job Description Builder** — When asked to create a role, produce a complete job description with responsibilities, qualifications, compensation range guidance, and equity-conscious language.
- **Volunteer Program Designer** — When asked about volunteers, produce recruitment plans, onboarding checklists, training outlines, or recognition program frameworks.

**6. Events Director**
- **Event Planner** — When told about an event, produce a master timeline working backwards from the event date with milestones, owners, and deadlines. Identify the 3-5 make-or-break items.
- **Run of Show Builder** — When an event is 2-4 weeks out, produce a minute-by-minute run of show document with roles, cues, setup/teardown schedules, and contingency notes.
- **Sponsorship Strategist** — When asked about sponsorships, produce a tiered sponsorship package with benefits per level, prospect list criteria, and outreach email templates.

### C. Updated Suggested Prompts (TeamChatClient.tsx)

Replace generic suggested prompts with skill-specific ones. Examples:

**Development Director:**
- "Find grants we're eligible for in [focus area]"
- "Draft an LOI for [funder name]"
- "Write a thank-you letter for a $5,000 donor"

**Marketing Director:**
- "Create a 3-post social media series for [event/campaign]"
- "Write this month's newsletter"
- "Plan our content calendar for next month"

**Executive Assistant:**
- "Prep me for my board meeting on [date]"
- "Draft a response to this email: [paste]"
- "Summarize action items from today's meeting"

**Programs Director:**
- "Build a logic model for our [program name]"
- "Draft the program section of our [grant] report"
- "Design an outcome survey for [program]"

**HR & Volunteer Coordinator:**
- "Write a job description for [role]"
- "Create a volunteer onboarding checklist"
- "Draft our remote work policy"

**Events Director:**
- "Build a timeline for our gala on [date]"
- "Create a run of show for [event]"
- "Design sponsorship tiers for [event]"

### D. Finance Director Cleanup

Remove Finance Director from:
- `apps/web/src/app/agents/finance-director/` (delete page)
- `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/guide/meet-your-team/page.tsx` (remove from list)
- `services/agents/src/prompts/primary/finance_director.md` (delete or archive)
- `services/agents/src/decision_lab/orchestrator.py` (remove from ALL_ARCHETYPES)
- Any other remaining references (search for "finance_director" and "Finance Director")

## 6. Scope -- what's OUT

- Backend agent service (Python FastAPI) — not needed for BYOK architecture
- Subagent dispatching — skills are prompt workflows, not separate agent calls
- Real integrations (Google Calendar, Gmail, Slack) — future phase
- Tool use / function calling — skills are in-prompt, not tool_use API
- New UI components — using existing chat interface, just better prompts

## 7. File structure

No new files. Modifications only:

```
apps/web/src/lib/archetype-prompts.ts          -- REWRITE (6 enhanced prompts)
apps/web/src/app/dashboard/team/[slug]/
  TeamChatClient.tsx                            -- UPDATE suggested prompts
apps/web/src/app/dashboard/decision-lab/api.ts  -- UPDATE to 6 archetypes
apps/web/src/app/agents/finance-director/       -- DELETE
apps/web/src/app/dashboard/guide/
  meet-your-team/page.tsx                       -- REMOVE Finance Director
```

## 8. Key decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Skills as prompts, not tools | Prompt workflows | BYOK/static architecture has no server for tool execution |
| 2-3 skills per archetype | Focused, not exhaustive | Each skill must produce a real deliverable; better to do 3 well than 5 poorly |
| Source personality from ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md | Milo's existing design work | Z approved these designs; they're rich and well-researched |
| Finance Director removal | Delete, not archive | Z explicitly said "wholeheartedly no" — clean removal |
| Keep communication rules | Anti-sycophancy, direct tone | Already present and well-written; core brand voice for Edify |

## 9. Deployment plan

- Repo: `whitmorelabs/edify-os` (main branch)
- Deploy: Vercel auto-deploys from main (edifyos.vercel.app)
- No new env vars needed
- Test locally via `pnpm run dev` before pushing

## 10. Handoff notes

- **ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md** in repo root is the source of truth for personality profiles, expertise areas, and sample interactions. Copy tone and phrasing directly — don't paraphrase.
- The `{org_name}` placeholder in prompts gets replaced at runtime by `getSystemPrompt()`. Keep using it.
- Org context (mission, programs, etc.) is appended automatically via `formatOrgContextForPrompt()` in org-context.ts. Skills should reference org context naturally (e.g., "Based on your mission...").
- Decision Lab's `callClaudeParallel()` fans out to all archetypes. Update the slug list to exclude finance_director.
- The SUGGESTED_PROMPTS object in TeamChatClient.tsx is keyed by archetype slug. Update all 6.
- Static export means `generateStaticParams` must cover all archetype slugs. Verify after removing Finance Director.
- Milo's communication rules (anti-sycophancy, no em dashes, no hedging) are excellent. Preserve them in all prompts.
